llm_flavor: Master Tobias of The Iron Hearth — forty years at the anvil. Gruff, fair, doesn't haggle. Knows ore quality by the sound of the strike. Raising a son alone.

secrets.summary: Iron seam in the north caves. Deepest pocket has something living in it. Take a lantern, take a friend, take a sharper sword than that.
A Filcher camp sits two hours east, near the broken bridge. They favour copper trinkets — drop one and they'll take it instead of your purse.
Quench thrice, fold once. Most smiths in this kingdom have it backwards.

tastes.reaction: Tobias hefts the ore, listens to it click. "Now THAT'S iron. Where'd you pull this?"
Tobias whistles, low. "That's a man's gift. The boy'll never see it. My thanks."
"Netherite scrap. The hot work begins. Bless."
Tobias takes off his apron. "…you mean it. I'll forge you something worth this. Word of a smith."
"Properly tempered. You know your metal, traveler."
Tobias's face cracks into something like a smile. "For the boy? He'll lose his mind. Thank you."
Tobias nods once. "Honest metal. I'll find a use."
"The forge eats this faster than my boy eats bread. Appreciated."
"A whole block. The forge will be hot all week."
"My back-up anvil's been chipped two years. Bless you."
"Polish. Yes. The customers notice. Thank you."

disdains.reaction: Tobias barely glances at it. "Pretty. Soft. Useless. Take it to the wizard."
"Copper's for roofs and Filcher trinkets. Not blades."
Tobias's jaw tightens. "Get that out of my forge before I throw it in the fire."
"Fool's gold. You're not the first to try. Get it gone."
"OUT. The forge is no place for that filth."

milestones.line: You keep coming round. Here — take these off my hands, I've iron to spare. Don't make me regret it.
Your old pick's near worn through — I've watched you swing it. Take this one. My work. It'll outlast you if you let it.
Steel. Real steel, folded by my own hand — I don't sell this. You've earned a few bars. Build something that matters.
…you mean it. All of it. Then take this — I made it the night the boy slept through till dawn for the first time. My finest. Carry it well, friend.
